articles.title: JSIR: A High-Level IR for JavaScript from Google
articles.description: Google has open sourced a new tool (JSIR) and proposed an industry-standard IR (Intermediate Representation – if an AST tells you what the code looks like, an IR tells you what it does) for JavaScript. Already used at Google for analysis and code transformation, the underlying idea could form a foundation for a new generation of tooling.
articles.author: Zhixun Tan (Google)

articles.title: What to Know in JavaScript (2026 Edition)
articles.description: An up-to-date overview of the JS landscape, including the latest ECMAScript additions, frameworks to keep tabs on, runtimes, build tools, and more. A good way to catch up.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: Minimum Release Age is an Underrated Supply Chain Defense
articles.description: An increasingly common package manager feature is being able to specify a minimum ‘package age’. The idea is that if you wait, then maintainers, security tools, etc. will tackle the most nefarious supply chain attacks. It’s no silver bullet, but may suit your use case, and here’s how to set it up.
articles.author: Dani Akash

articles.title: ▶  TanStack Start: A Client-First Web Framework
articles.description: A 30-minute talk from TanStack’s founder showcasing TanStack Start’s value proposition for both React and Solid developers looking for a complete SSR framework.
articles.author: Tanner Linsley

articles.title: Burnout is Real for Open Source Maintainers
articles.description: A 40-minute audio interview (along with a nice write up) with John-David Dalton, the creator of Lodash, one of JavaScript's most popular projects.
articles.author: The OpenJS Foundation

articles.title: The Great CSS Expansion
articles.description: A thorough review of Web-based tasks that were once JavaScript’s natural domain (e.g. tooltips, dialogs, scroll animations) but for which modern CSS now excels.
articles.author: Pavel Laptev

articles.title: Fuse.js 7.3: Lightweight Fuzzy-Search
articles.description: Want a search feature tolerant to ambiguous input without a dedicated backend? v7.3 adds per-term fuzzy matching and a static method for single string matching, while v7.4 beta adds worker-based distributed search for tackling huge datasets. A demo shows off the basics.
articles.author: Kiro Risk

articles.title: Announcing Babylon.js 9.0
articles.description: Microsoft's popular rendering engine for building interactive, 3D web experiences now has a node-based particle editor, volumetric lighting, advanced Gaussian splatting, and more.
articles.author: Carter & Lucchini (Microsoft)

articles.title: Marked.js 18.0: A Fast Markdown Parser and Compiler
articles.description: A low-level Markdown compiler built for speed. The demo shows off the basics. v18 is largely a bug fix release that also bumps it up to TypeScript 6. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Christopher Jeffrey

articles.title: TinyBase v8.1: A Reactive Data Store for Local-First Apps
articles.description: A reactive data store and sync engine that can be used as the entire backend for many types of app, now with native Svelte 5 support.
articles.author: James Pearce

articles.title: xdk-typescript: The Official 'XDK' for the X API
articles.description: The social media platform’s new official SDK for its API (good luck).
articles.author: X Dev Platform

articles.description: 🔓 The Axios team has published a postmortem of last week's npm supply chain compromise. There's also a look into how the social engineering part worked.
📊 WebKit, Google and Mozilla have unveiled JetStream 3, the latest version of a suite of popular browser-oriented JS and WASM performance benchmarks.
Cloudflare has released EmDash, a JavaScript-flavored 'spiritual successor to WordPress'.
The Svelte team has published its latest monthly roundup.
ESLint v10.2.0 – Adds support for language-aware rules through a new meta.languages property. Temporal is now also supported.
Node.js 25.9.0 (Current) – Adds --max-heap-size to set a max heap size for a process, and includes stream/iter, a new experimental iterable streams API.

articles.title: Introducing view-transitions-toolkit: Utility Functions to More Easily Work with View Transitions
articles.description: Bramus has been experimenting with View Transitions for a good while now. He's brought together his numerous code patterns into a new dedicated npm package full of view transition utilities, helpers, and functions. There’s a bunch of demos here, and the code is on GitHub.
articles.author: Bramus Van Damme (Google)

articles.title: 📊 Announcing JetStream 3.0
articles.description: Seven years after v2 comes a big update to the popular cross-browser JS/WASM benchmark suite. It’s a group effort, with work from Mozilla, WebKit, and Chromium, with updates to the workloads and methodology. The tool is here, as well as more detail on the analysis performed.
articles.author: Browser Bench

articles.title: How to Use HTML Video and Audio Lazy-Loading
articles.description: HTML video and audio lazy loading is now a web standard. It’ll be available in Chrome 148 and Firefox and WebKit implementations are close behind. Here’s how it works.
articles.author: Scott Jehl (Squarespace)

articles.title: The WebAIM Million 2026 Report
articles.description: This accessibility analysis of a million sites makes for disappointing reading: despite years of progress, we’re now seeing web accessibility in decline. Why? They say “increased reliance on 3rd party frameworks and libraries and automated or AI-assisted coding practices”.
articles.author: WebAIM

articles.title: Alternatives to the !important Keyword
articles.description: When looking over old CSS you may encounter !important and forgot why you needed it. If you’re looking to fix up such debt, this post offers some modern alternative options.
articles.author: Saleh Mubashar

articles.title: CSS subgrid is Super Good
articles.description: CMS-driven layouts can often get a bit tricky, particularly when trying to ‘break out’ of set container widths. Dave shows how subgrid can simplify things by way of full-width or inset components, without the need for margin hacks or wrapper bloat.
articles.author: David Bushell

articles.title: Email Address Obfuscation in HTML: What Works in 2026?
articles.description: A test of numerous techniques to see which trip up the bots. It turns out most scrapers are very lazy, but there are a few techniques worth avoiding in 2026 (using HTML entities and URL encoding).
articles.author: Spencer Mortensen

articles.title: What to Know in JavaScript (2026 Edition)
articles.description: A high-level overview of the sprawling JavaScript landscape as it stands today, including a look at recent language additions, frameworks to keep tabs on, runtimes, build tools, etc.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: Name-Only Containers: The Scoping We Needed
articles.description: Chris explores a novel use for name-only containers as a way to solve CSS scoping challenges in a native way. Of course, we do have @scope at our disposal, but this is a curious alternative approach.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: New CSS Multi-Column Layout Features in Chrome
articles.description: Chrome 145 introduces support for a couple of new column-wrapping features, namely column-wrap and column-height. They’re a big upgrade for multi-column layouts, so here’s a look at how you may want to use them.
articles.author: Abhishek Pratap Singh

articles.title: Crashcat: A Physics Engine for JavaScript, Built for Games, Simulations, And Creative Websites
articles.description: The demo site has lots of impressive examples and the library has an extensive feature set for doing just about anything you’d need in a game or physics simulation.
articles.author: Isaac Mason

articles.title: Bearnie: Accessible Components for Astro and Tailwind CSS
articles.description: Promoted as a way to start your own component library, with ability to fully customize these, which follow WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines, including keyboard nav, screen reader support, and focus management. Docs site is here.
articles.author: Michael Andreuzza

articles.title: Dither Image: Web Tool to Add a Retro Pixel Art Effect to Images
articles.description: Includes a before/after toggle along with multiple settings to define the exact effect you want. A nice, quick alternative to more complex graphics tools. I just tried it with a shot from the Artemis II Moon mission. →
articles.author: Dither Image

articles.title: SSGOI: Native App-Like Page Transitions for the Web
articles.description: Boasts better browser support than native page transitions and it’s available for React/Next, Vue/Nuxt, Svelte, and Angular, with any routing system. You can check out some neat demos on the docs site.
articles.author: meursyphus

articles.title: MonoSketch: An ASCII Sketching and Diagramming App
articles.description: A neat, open source sketching/diagramming tool that lets you turn your ideas into ASCII designs. You can try it here.
articles.author: Tuan Chau

articles.title: Wiretext: A Unicode Wireframe Design Tool
articles.description: Similar to above, but works like a typical GUI tool with the end results being Unicode you can copy/paste. Includes numerous ‘components’ you can insert that we don’t normally associate with text (e.g. image, avatar, radio buttons, etc).
articles.author: Daniel Howells
